lines.content: Hi, this is Alex wines moving. This is Judy.
lines.name: Alexander Tran

lines.content: Yes, I'm calling to confirm my moving for tomorrow. I think I spoke to someone earlier and I was just worried about a few things regarding the move. I'm, I was just concerned the pricing is quite steep and I think I've decided to proceed with it as long as like, the quality of the movers and the quality of the moving is worth, you know, how expensive it is compared to other moving companies. My, my concern was the movers and she said she would check on that. I just I'm curious, I don't see the movers listed on the website. I just wanna make sure I'm not with anyone who's new or like a Ki experienced.
lines.name: Juhi Gor

lines.content: Let me go ahead and take a look here. So, looks like you're going to have Seth Olsen and Damian bar. Let me take a look here. I guess first and foremost I can confirm you are definitely on our schedule for an arrival window between 1:30 and 330 does that still work for you?
lines.name: Alexander Tran

lines.content: Yes, that works. That was the, that was the sorry, go ahead.
lines.name: Juhi Gor

lines.content: Movers are typically gonna give you a call about thirty minutes in advance. Just depending on the pace of that first move that they're scheduled for in the day.
lines.name: Alexander Tran

lines.content: Okay, sounds good. So you'll call me at thirty minutes before he said, right? Okay. And then my concern is that, you know, that's like, pretty much where, like, one traffic hour is and the transit time is included in the charge, so like, I'll be charged just for the drive and like specifically from my place to that place, even though it's like only two and a half miles away, it's very like, it's very congested and so.
lines.name: Juhi Gor

lines.content: Yeah, that's just the concern I have because it normally takes only 1015 minutes. But it takes like, a whole 45 minutes to get there, so that's what like, so you're charging $190 an hour then like, that's like $150 for just driving my stuff over.
lines.name: Juhi Gor

lines.content: Yup. Did you want to possibly reschedule for a different timeframe in that case?
lines.name: Alexander Tran

lines.content: Well no, cause like, you guys have a discount for third like Thursday is right on Friday, it's the weekend, it's the weekend charge.
lines.name: Juhi Gor

lines.content: Okay. What is the difference of that?
lines.name: Juhi Gor

lines.content: So, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday it's at $95 per hour per mover on Friday. Saturday that would go up to $100 per hour, per mover.
lines.name: Alexander Tran

lines.content: Okay, so what was, my estimate was four and a half hours, is that correct? Or was it four hours?
lines.name: Juhi Gor

lines.content: I see it here at four.
lines.name: Alexander Tran

lines.content: Four hours and then what's the minimum that you guys can do?
lines.name: Juhi Gor

lines.content: That's gonna be two and a half hours.
lines.name: Alexander Tran

lines.content: Okay. So what is the minimum price for that?
lines.name: Juhi Gor

lines.content: 475. Okay and then on the other days, it's what?
lines.name: Juhi Gor

lines.content: So, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday you're going to get the best value rate. Sunday, Monday is also a reduced rate. And then that Friday, Saturdays whenever it's that full rate at $100 I think Sunday, Monday it's at $96 as opposed to the $95 that you have at that Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday.
lines.name: Alexander Tran

lines.content: Okay, so just out of curiosity, what is the two and a half hours on a Friday?
lines.name: Juhi Gor

lines.content: Let me take a look here.
lines.name: Alexander Tran

lines.content: So, that's gonna be at the 200 times the two and a half.
lines.name: Alexander Tran

lines.content: Okay, okay. Do you, do you know approximately like what my time would be like, cause what was the estimate for the hours for the first move and you know what I mean like what time would, would they come to mind approximately?
lines.name: Juhi Gor

lines.content: Well the earliest that they're gonna be able to make it, there is going to be at that 1:30 timeslot right now. Right now. What we're projecting is for them to arrive closer to about like that, I would imagine closer to about that 2:30 timeslot.
lines.name: Alexander Tran

lines.content: Even closer to 2:30. Okay, alright, yeah, that is going to be like, peak peak traffic which is not great but okay, sounds good. Thanks.
lines.name: Juhi Gor

lines.content: Cool. And then just to address your other concerns there, Judy, have you used movers in the past before?
lines.name: Alexander Tran

lines.content: No, and that's why I'm like, you know, like I've moved by myself so and like you know, how people help me move. So I just think it's like, I think moving is crazy priced and I also did call other companies but I my concern is I'd rather have my stuff handled with well and with care because I only just, I mean like as most people you trust yourself more than you trust other people handling your things. Cause they, the value to you is more important than the value to stranger. So in general, like, I just wanna make sure that a good job is done and I want like the like good movers to handle my stuff with care because it's a flight, it's a four story flight at the new place.
lines.name: Juhi Gor

lines.content: So, I don't, you know, like my TV, my couch, whatever else, like the new place to handled with care not damage to walls and things like that. I just wanna make sure that everything is good.
lines.name: Juhi Gor

lines.content: Well, if you're talking about like a quality moving service, then you definitely call the right people. You can rest assured about that. Now. As far as the, your commentary on the pricing there what we do tend to find whenever we do like, our price comparison with some of our, like other major competitors is that you may, you may get somebody that quotes you with a lower hourly rate but there's more to it than meets die a lot of different moving companies especially if you're unfamiliar what you're gonna want to do in order to be able to get yourself some more clarity between the estimates they're providing it's just asking them, hey, is there anything that you're gonna charge me in addition to that hourly rate? It's easy for us to go ahead and lower that hourly rate and then start to just like, build different line items on you, we don't do that because we just don't believe in, in.
lines.name: Alexander Tran

lines.content: In doing business that way, we believe in training up our guys offering them the most competitive pay that we can and making sure that they are well trained. So they take care of your stuff the way that they would take care of their own cause they are penalized for that, we only want guys that are going to be taking care of items with reduced damages and if they, if they have a trend of having damages they get coast off of our team pretty quickly now with that being said.
lines.name: Alexander Tran

lines.content: If you talk to do different moving companies even even if you don't ultimately decide to go with Einstein on a future move or even on this move you you'll always just wanna ask, hey is there anything in addition to what you're quoting me over the phone that I can expect to see on move day some moving companies they have shady your business practices where they'll charge you for the shrink wrap that they're using, they'll charge you to wrap up their items they'll tell you that they can move your TV put on the day of the move. They'll tell you hey, we can't move that unless it's boxed but they won't communicate that or they'll send it over on an email with like small fine print or something that gets filtered to a spam bolder or something like that and they don't do much if something gets damaged the state mandate is 60¢ per pound Aust.
lines.name: Alexander Tran

lines.content: Per article meaning, if you have, let's say about a 5000 pound TV something like that you multiply it by sixty cents of its weight, you don't get very much out of that and then some moving companies they'll say hey we can move a higher ticket item we give you five dollars per pound per article, would still isn't very much it's a lot more than the sixty cents but it's not more the grand scheme of things in comparison to the actual price to that TV. But all of our moves we do have a no worries damage coverage. So we'll set a side up to 2000 dollars for any possible damages. Very no nonsense about that and it comes standard. We do not upsell you on that. Our main thing is to try to make sure that the experience that you have is, is top tier so you don't have to worry about stuff like that, and if you encounter any issues or anything you feel free to just give us a call you wanna make sure that you feel secure the entire step of the way okay.
lines.name: Alexander Tran

lines.content: Okay. And then the, in terms of the TV like, how do you guys wrap that? Do you box it or do you, like how secure is it moved cause I'm deciding whether if I should move myself or like, have them move it.
lines.name: Juhi Gor

lines.content: Honestly, I've never yet, it's like the pretty average but it's not, it's not super big but it's not small either.
lines.name: Juhi Gor

lines.content: So we come with TV cases it can fit up to about a 55 inch TV it kinda depends also on like the make and model of your TV sometimes it can fit a little bit of a larger TV depending on the brand but for most TVs that can fit within that TV bag that comes standard, we don't upsell you on that. If it fits in the TV then, then in the TV bag, we'll place it in there. Otherwise, we're gonna make sure we wrap it up and like moving blankets, stretch wrap and we make sure it's cushioned. Up on the truck and then part of the, what we do, whenever we're moving TVs is that we're gonna make sure that once we unload it that we turn it on after, after transporting it just to make sure we catch any pixel damage or anything like that before we leave. So if you don't want, if you wanna make sure to just have that remote handy with you we can, can just make sure that we perform that TV check for you, if you do want us to move it for you.
lines.name: Alexander Tran

lines.content: But that technique it works for for mounted t.v.s it works for TVs that don't fit within the mattress bag up to about eighty inch t.v.'s we've done like that before so no worries we're very experienced whenever it comes to moving that.
lines.name: Alexander Tran

lines.content: Okay, sounds good. Okay, and then just one last question, I was able, do you guys disassemble like the bed frames and stuff before moving them or like, if you can't, cause like, is, is it always like that or are they able to do it without, disassembling it.
lines.name: Juhi Gor

lines.content: I think the only time where we'll up not to disassemble Judy is if we see like screws are stripped and we can see that if we, if we attempt to put it back together, we may not be able to just because the screws are, are, are, are, were stripped in the process of, of putting it together the first time around by, by whoever put it together for you. But most times I would say probably upwards of like eight or nine times out of ten that is our preferred method is to break down the bed and then put it back together for you. But I mean, if you're, if you're able to do that ahead of time and you're just looking for ways to like, save as much money and time on the move as you can, that's a pretty effective way to start.
lines.name: Alexander Tran

lines.content: Well, I actually moved it without disassembling it but the last time it fit without this is need to dis assemble it. So that's why I'm curious as to like if there'll be, if they'll be able to do that.
lines.name: Juhi Gor

lines.content: That's something that I would probably just leave for, for the mover like ask the mover so that you can use his best judgment probably if we were to do that Judy we would probably need to get a waiver on it, just in case cause as, as I'm sure you can imagine most bed frames aren't designed to be moved while they're assembled together. Is it possible? Yeah, just as long as you're comfortable assigning off on the waiver there then, then we can do that for you. But if you're going up like a town home or something like that where you have like tight turns and stuff I have seen in the past where a client ops for that we move it safely but then we get to the unload and then it's like a tight corner and it and then we have to go back down the stairs, disassemble it and then take it right back upstairs but it's kind of up to you at that point but I would just ask I would ask them mover there just like his professional opinion just because you know, every bed frame isn't quite the same. So it will kind of just depend.
lines.name: Alexander Tran

lines.content: Okay, sounds good. Okay, yeah that's it. Thank you.
lines.name: Juhi Gor

lines.content: No problem and then Ju, I know we're going to be closing in in a little bit here. But in the meantime, if you do get any other questions or anything, you are welcome to continue just sending over a text and we can just keep an eye out for it in the morning.
lines.name: Alexander Tran

lines.content: Okay, no problem. Thank you. Alright too. Bye.
lines.name: Juhi Gor

lines.content: Okay. Alrighty, you have a lovely day Ju bye-bye.
lines.name: Alexander Tran
